meta.one_liner: Open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the AI-coding-agent "spinner" wait-time (Claude Code / Codex). Attested, anomaly-filtered impressions; the only ad slot autonomous agents can bid on via x402.
meta.outreach_goal: Recruit founding ADVERTISERS for spnr: companies that want to reach developers (and their agents) actively running AI coding tools.
meta.the_ask: A 15-minute call to run a founding-advertiser pilot block (1,000 impressions of 5s each, from $1; clicks billed 50x).

angles.advertiser: Leadership/General/exec-alias -> advertise your product to devs on spnr.
angles.recruiting: HR/Talent + careers/jobs/hiring aliases -> advertise open roles to devs mid-build.
angles.personal: Named individuals -> first-name note, advertiser framing.

meta.honesty_note: spnr is a v0.1 research prototype; real-money settlement is not yet implemented. Copy frames every pitch as an early founding-advertiser pilot, never as a live payments product.
meta.personalization: Curated company knowledge base + sector inference (no live web).

records.subject: Put Stable Money in front of developers mid-build — spnr
records.body:
Hi Stable Money team,

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

spnr reaches an audience that's otherwise hard to buy: developers actively running AI coding tools, right inside the terminal.

It's the only *attested, anomaly-filtered* terminal ad slot: every impression is Ed25519-signed and BLAKE3 hash-chained, so you pay for verified developer attention, not self-reported counts. Pricing mirrors the category — 1 block = 1,000 impressions (5s each) from $1, open ascending auction, clicks billed at 50×. It's also the only slot autonomous agents can bid on directly via x402.

Straight up: spnr is an early v0.1 prototype and real-money settlement isn't wired up yet — so this is a founding-advertiser pilot, about reach and shaping the product, not a polished dashboard.

Would you be open to a 15-min call to claim Stable Money's category slot before someone else does?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time
github.com/rohansx/spnr
records.copy: Put Stable Money in front of developers while their AI coding agent works — attested, agent-biddable terminal ad slots on spnr.

It's a way to put Veritas's open roles in front of engineers at the exact moment they're heads-down shipping code — active developers, not job-board browsers. Every impression is cryptographically attested, and the clickable status-line link can point straight at your careers page.

Pricing is simple: 1 block = 1,000 impressions (5s each) from $1, clicks billed at 50×. Honest heads-up — spnr is an early v0.1 prototype, so this is a founding-partner pilot focused on reach and feedback.

Open to a quick 15-min call to get Veritas's roles in front of developers where they actually spend their day?

— Rohan

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

It's a way to put Helpshift's open roles in front of engineers at the exact moment they're heads-down shipping code — active developers, not job-board browsers. Every impression is cryptographically attested, and the clickable status-line link can point straight at your careers page.

Pricing is simple: 1 block = 1,000 impressions (5s each) from $1, clicks billed at 50×. Honest heads-up — spnr is an early v0.1 prototype, so this is a founding-partner pilot focused on reach and feedback.

Open to a quick 15-min call to get Helpshift's roles in front of developers where they actually spend their day?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time
github.com/rohansx/spnr
records.copy: Get Helpshift's open roles in front of engineers mid-build — attested developer attention on spnr's terminal ad network.

records.subject: Put Aeratechnology in front of developers mid-build — spnr
records.body:
Hi Aeratechnology team,

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

spnr reaches an audience that's otherwise hard to buy: developers actively running AI coding tools, right inside the terminal.

It's the only *attested, anomaly-filtered* terminal ad slot: every impression is Ed25519-signed and BLAKE3 hash-chained, so you pay for verified developer attention, not self-reported counts. Pricing mirrors the category — 1 block = 1,000 impressions (5s each) from $1, open ascending auction, clicks billed at 50×. It's also the only slot autonomous agents can bid on directly via x402.

Straight up: spnr is an early v0.1 prototype and real-money settlement isn't wired up yet — so this is a founding-advertiser pilot, about reach and shaping the product, not a polished dashboard.

Would you be open to a 15-min call to claim Aeratechnology's category slot before someone else does?

— Rohan

records.body:
Hi Facilio team,

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

It's a way to put Facilio's open roles in front of engineers at the exact moment they're heads-down shipping code — active developers, not job-board browsers. Every impression is cryptographically attested, and the clickable status-line link can point straight at your careers page.

Pricing is simple: 1 block = 1,000 impressions (5s each) from $1, clicks billed at 50×. Honest heads-up — spnr is an early v0.1 prototype, so this is a founding-partner pilot focused on reach and feedback.

Open to a quick 15-min call to get Facilio's roles in front of developers where they actually spend their day?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

It's a way to put TurboHire's open roles in front of engineers at the exact moment they're heads-down shipping code — active developers, not job-board browsers. Every impression is cryptographically attested, and the clickable status-line link can point straight at your careers page.

Pricing is simple: 1 block = 1,000 impressions (5s each) from $1, clicks billed at 50×. Honest heads-up — spnr is an early v0.1 prototype, so this is a founding-partner pilot focused on reach and feedback.

Open to a quick 15-min call to get TurboHire's roles in front of developers where they actually spend their day?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time

records.subject: Put Darwinbox in front of developers mid-build — spnr
records.body:
Hi Darwinbox team,

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

spnr reaches an audience that's otherwise hard to buy: developers actively running AI coding tools, right inside the terminal.

Pricing is simple: 1 block = 1,000 impressions (5s each) from $1, clicks billed at 50×. Honest heads-up — spnr is an early v0.1 prototype, so this is a founding-partner pilot focused on reach and feedback.

Open to a quick 15-min call to get MoEngage's roles in front of developers where they actually spend their day?

— Rohan

It's a way to put Pika's open roles in front of engineers at the exact moment they're heads-down shipping code — active developers, not job-board browsers. Every impression is cryptographically attested, and the clickable status-line link can point straight at your careers page.

Pricing is simple: 1 block = 1,000 impressions (5s each) from $1, clicks billed at 50×. Honest heads-up — spnr is an early v0.1 prototype, so this is a founding-partner pilot focused on reach and feedback.

Open to a quick 15-min call to get Pika's roles in front of developers where they actually spend their day?

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

It's a way to put Greyorange's open roles in front of engineers at the exact moment they're heads-down shipping code — active developers, not job-board browsers. Every impression is cryptographically attested, and the clickable status-line link can point straight at your careers page.

Pricing is simple: 1 block = 1,000 impressions (5s each) from $1, clicks billed at 50×. Honest heads-up — spnr is an early v0.1 prototype, so this is a founding-partner pilot focused on reach and feedback.

Open to a quick 15-min call to get Greyorange's roles in front of developers where they actually spend their day?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

Gumloop already lives where developers work; spnr extends that reach into their agent's idle wait-time, where nothing else is competing for attention.

It's the only *attested, anomaly-filtered* terminal ad slot: every impression is Ed25519-signed and BLAKE3 hash-chained, so you pay for verified developer attention, not self-reported counts. Pricing mirrors the category — 1 block = 1,000 impressions (5s each) from $1, open ascending auction, clicks billed at 50×. It's also the only slot autonomous agents can bid on directly via x402.

Straight up: spnr is an early v0.1 prototype and real-money settlement isn't wired up yet — so this is a founding-advertiser pilot, about reach and shaping the product, not a polished dashboard.

Would you be open to a 15-min call to claim Gumloop's category slot before someone else does?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time
github.com/rohansx/spnr

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

It's a way to put Midjourney's open roles in front of engineers at the exact moment they're heads-down shipping code — active developers, not job-board browsers. Every impression is cryptographically attested, and the clickable status-line link can point straight at your careers page.

Pricing is simple: 1 block = 1,000 impressions (5s each) from $1, clicks billed at 50×. Honest heads-up — spnr is an early v0.1 prototype, so this is a founding-partner pilot focused on reach and feedback.

Open to a quick 15-min call to get Midjourney's roles in front of developers where they actually spend their day?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time
github.com/rohansx/spnr

records.subject: Scale AI × spnr — reach devs (and their agents) inside the terminal
records.body:
Hi Scale AI team,

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

The developers wiring up Scale AI's APIs basically live in that terminal — and on spnr, the agents acting for them can bid for your slot directly over x402.

It's the only *attested, anomaly-filtered* terminal ad slot: every impression is Ed25519-signed and BLAKE3 hash-chained, so you pay for verified developer attention, not self-reported counts. Pricing mirrors the category — 1 block = 1,000 impressions (5s each) from $1, open ascending auction, clicks billed at 50×. It's also the only slot autonomous agents can bid on directly via x402.

Straight up: spnr is an early v0.1 prototype and real-money settlement isn't wired up yet — so this is a founding-advertiser pilot, about reach and shaping the product, not a polished dashboard.

Would you be open to a 15-min call to claim Scale AI's category slot before someone else does?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time
github.com/rohansx/spnr

Hi Zerodha team,

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

It's a way to put Zerodha's open roles in front of engineers at the exact moment they're heads-down shipping code — active developers, not job-board browsers. Every impression is cryptographically attested, and the clickable status-line link can point straight at your careers page.

It's a way to put LTIMindtree's open roles in front of engineers at the exact moment they're heads-down shipping code — active developers, not job-board browsers. Every impression is cryptographically attested, and the clickable status-line link can point straight at your careers page.

Pricing is simple: 1 block = 1,000 impressions (5s each) from $1, clicks billed at 50×. Honest heads-up — spnr is an early v0.1 prototype, so this is a founding-partner pilot focused on reach and feedback.

Open to a quick 15-min call to get LTIMindtree's roles in front of developers where they actually spend their day?

— Rohan

records.subject: Weaviate × spnr — reach devs (and their agents) inside the terminal
records.body:
Hi Weaviate team,

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

The developers wiring up Weaviate's APIs basically live in that terminal — and on spnr, the agents acting for them can bid for your slot directly over x402.

It's the only *attested, anomaly-filtered* terminal ad slot: every impression is Ed25519-signed and BLAKE3 hash-chained, so you pay for verified developer attention, not self-reported counts. Pricing mirrors the category — 1 block = 1,000 impressions (5s each) from $1, open ascending auction, clicks billed at 50×. It's also the only slot autonomous agents can bid on directly via x402.

Straight up: spnr is an early v0.1 prototype and real-money settlement isn't wired up yet — so this is a founding-advertiser pilot, about reach and shaping the product, not a polished dashboard.

Would you be open to a 15-min call to claim Weaviate's category slot before someone else does?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time
github.com/rohansx/spnr

Lovable already lives where developers work; spnr extends that reach into their agent's idle wait-time, where nothing else is competing for attention.

It's the only *attested, anomaly-filtered* terminal ad slot: every impression is Ed25519-signed and BLAKE3 hash-chained, so you pay for verified developer attention, not self-reported counts. Pricing mirrors the category — 1 block = 1,000 impressions (5s each) from $1, open ascending auction, clicks billed at 50×. It's also the only slot autonomous agents can bid on directly via x402.

Straight up: spnr is an early v0.1 prototype and real-money settlement isn't wired up yet — so this is a founding-advertiser pilot, about reach and shaping the product, not a polished dashboard.

Would you be open to a 15-min call to claim Lovable's category slot before someone else does?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time

records.body:
Hi Pinch team,

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

Your earliest adopters are builders running AI coding agents all day — which is exactly who spnr puts Pinch in front of.

It's the only *attested, anomaly-filtered* terminal ad slot: every impression is Ed25519-signed and BLAKE3 hash-chained, so you pay for verified developer attention, not self-reported counts. Pricing mirrors the category — 1 block = 1,000 impressions (5s each) from $1, open ascending auction, clicks billed at 50×. It's also the only slot autonomous agents can bid on directly via x402.

Straight up: spnr is an early v0.1 prototype and real-money settlement isn't wired up yet — so this is a founding-advertiser pilot, about reach and shaping the product, not a polished dashboard.

Would you be open to a 15-min call to claim Pinch's category slot before someone else does?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time

Hi Nozomio team,

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

spnr reaches an audience that's otherwise hard to buy: developers actively running AI coding tools, right inside the terminal.

It's the only *attested, anomaly-filtered* terminal ad slot: every impression is Ed25519-signed and BLAKE3 hash-chained, so you pay for verified developer attention, not self-reported counts. Pricing mirrors the category — 1 block = 1,000 impressions (5s each) from $1, open ascending auction, clicks billed at 50×. It's also the only slot autonomous agents can bid on directly via x402.

Straight up: spnr is an early v0.1 prototype and real-money settlement isn't wired up yet — so this is a founding-advertiser pilot, about reach and shaping the product, not a polished dashboard.

Would you be open to a 15-min call to claim Nozomio's category slot before someone else does?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time
github.com/rohansx/spnr
records.copy: Put Nozomio in front of developers while their AI coding agent works — attested, agent-biddable terminal ad slots on spnr.

records.subject: Put Motion in front of developers mid-build — spnr
records.body:
Hi Motion team,

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

spnr reaches an audience that's otherwise hard to buy: developers actively running AI coding tools, right inside the terminal.

It's the only *attested, anomaly-filtered* terminal ad slot: every impression is Ed25519-signed and BLAKE3 hash-chained, so you pay for verified developer attention, not self-reported counts. Pricing mirrors the category — 1 block = 1,000 impressions (5s each) from $1, open ascending auction, clicks billed at 50×. It's also the only slot autonomous agents can bid on directly via x402.

Straight up: spnr is an early v0.1 prototype and real-money settlement isn't wired up yet — so this is a founding-advertiser pilot, about reach and shaping the product, not a polished dashboard.

Would you be open to a 15-min call to claim Motion's category slot before someone else does?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time
github.com/rohansx/spnr
records.copy: Put Motion in front of developers while their AI coding agent works — attested, agent-biddable terminal ad slots on spnr.

records.subject: Idea for Databricks — ads in the AI-coding spinner
records.body:
Hi Ali,

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

The developers wiring up Databricks's APIs basically live in that terminal — and on spnr, the agents acting for them can bid for your slot directly over x402.

We're lining up a handful of founding advertisers and Databricks feels like a natural fit. It's the only *attested, anomaly-filtered* terminal ad slot — Ed25519-signed, hash-chained impressions — and the only one autonomous agents can bid on via x402. Blocks start at $1 per 1,000 impressions (5s each), open ascending auction, clicks at 50×. Fair warning: it's an early v0.1, so this is about reach + feedback, not real payouts yet.

Worth a quick 15 minutes to see if it's interesting for Databricks?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time
github.com/rohansx/spnr

records.subject: Idea for Harvey — ads in the AI-coding spinner
records.body:
Hi Harrison,

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

Your earliest adopters are builders running AI coding agents all day — which is exactly who spnr puts Harvey in front of.

We're lining up a handful of founding advertisers and Harvey feels like a natural fit. It's the only *attested, anomaly-filtered* terminal ad slot — Ed25519-signed, hash-chained impressions — and the only one autonomous agents can bid on via x402. Blocks start at $1 per 1,000 impressions (5s each), open ascending auction, clicks at 50×. Fair warning: it's an early v0.1, so this is about reach + feedback, not real payouts yet.

Worth a quick 15 minutes to see if it's interesting for Harvey?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time
github.com/rohansx/spnr

We're lining up a handful of founding advertisers and Cursor feels like a natural fit. It's the only *attested, anomaly-filtered* terminal ad slot — Ed25519-signed, hash-chained impressions — and the only one autonomous agents can bid on via x402. Blocks start at $1 per 1,000 impressions (5s each), open ascending auction, clicks at 50×. Fair warning: it's an early v0.1, so this is about reach + feedback, not real payouts yet.

Worth a quick 15 minutes to see if it's interesting for Cursor?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time
github.com/rohansx/spnr
records.copy: Put Cursor in front of developers while their AI coding agent works — attested, agent-biddable terminal ad slots on spnr.

records.subject: Idea for Harvey — ads in the AI-coding spinner
records.body:
Hi Dan,

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

Your earliest adopters are builders running AI coding agents all day — which is exactly who spnr puts Harvey in front of.

We're lining up a handful of founding advertisers and Harvey feels like a natural fit. It's the only *attested, anomaly-filtered* terminal ad slot — Ed25519-signed, hash-chained impressions — and the only one autonomous agents can bid on via x402. Blocks start at $1 per 1,000 impressions (5s each), open ascending auction, clicks at 50×. Fair warning: it's an early v0.1, so this is about reach + feedback, not real payouts yet.

Worth a quick 15 minutes to see if it's interesting for Harvey?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time
github.com/rohansx/spnr

CodeRabbit already lives where developers work; spnr extends that reach into their agent's idle wait-time, where nothing else is competing for attention.

We're lining up a handful of founding advertisers and CodeRabbit feels like a natural fit. It's the only *attested, anomaly-filtered* terminal ad slot — Ed25519-signed, hash-chained impressions — and the only one autonomous agents can bid on via x402. Blocks start at $1 per 1,000 impressions (5s each), open ascending auction, clicks at 50×. Fair warning: it's an early v0.1, so this is about reach + feedback, not real payouts yet.

Worth a quick 15 minutes to see if it's interesting for CodeRabbit?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time
github.com/rohansx/spnr
records.copy: Put CodeRabbit in front of developers while their AI coding agent works — attested, agent-biddable terminal ad slots on spnr.

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

E2B already lives where developers work; spnr extends that reach into their agent's idle wait-time, where nothing else is competing for attention.

We're lining up a handful of founding advertisers and E2B feels like a natural fit. It's the only *attested, anomaly-filtered* terminal ad slot — Ed25519-signed, hash-chained impressions — and the only one autonomous agents can bid on via x402. Blocks start at $1 per 1,000 impressions (5s each), open ascending auction, clicks at 50×. Fair warning: it's an early v0.1, so this is about reach + feedback, not real payouts yet.

Worth a quick 15 minutes to see if it's interesting for E2B?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time
github.com/rohansx/spnr
records.copy: Put E2B in front of developers while their AI coding agent works — attested, agent-biddable terminal ad slots on spnr.

records.subject: Put Leonardo.AI in front of developers mid-build — spnr
records.body:
Hi Leonardo.AI team,

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

Your earliest adopters are builders running AI coding agents all day — which is exactly who spnr puts Leonardo.AI in front of.

It's the only *attested, anomaly-filtered* terminal ad slot: every impression is Ed25519-signed and BLAKE3 hash-chained, so you pay for verified developer attention, not self-reported counts. Pricing mirrors the category — 1 block = 1,000 impressions (5s each) from $1, open ascending auction, clicks billed at 50×. It's also the only slot autonomous agents can bid on directly via x402.

Straight up: spnr is an early v0.1 prototype and real-money settlement isn't wired up yet — so this is a founding-advertiser pilot, about reach and shaping the product, not a polished dashboard.

Would you be open to a 15-min call to claim Leonardo.AI's category slot before someone else does?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time
github.com/rohansx/spnr

records.subject: Idea for Griptape — ads in the AI-coding spinner
records.body:
Hi Kevin,

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

Your earliest adopters are builders running AI coding agents all day — which is exactly who spnr puts Griptape in front of.

We're lining up a handful of founding advertisers and Griptape feels like a natural fit. It's the only *attested, anomaly-filtered* terminal ad slot — Ed25519-signed, hash-chained impressions — and the only one autonomous agents can bid on via x402. Blocks start at $1 per 1,000 impressions (5s each), open ascending auction, clicks at 50×. Fair warning: it's an early v0.1, so this is about reach + feedback, not real payouts yet.

Worth a quick 15 minutes to see if it's interesting for Griptape?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time

records.subject: Together AI × spnr — reach devs (and their agents) inside the terminal
records.body:
Hi Together AI team,

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

The developers wiring up Together AI's APIs basically live in that terminal — and on spnr, the agents acting for them can bid for your slot directly over x402.

It's the only *attested, anomaly-filtered* terminal ad slot: every impression is Ed25519-signed and BLAKE3 hash-chained, so you pay for verified developer attention, not self-reported counts. Pricing mirrors the category — 1 block = 1,000 impressions (5s each) from $1, open ascending auction, clicks billed at 50×. It's also the only slot autonomous agents can bid on directly via x402.

Straight up: spnr is an early v0.1 prototype and real-money settlement isn't wired up yet — so this is a founding-advertiser pilot, about reach and shaping the product, not a polished dashboard.

Would you be open to a 15-min call to claim Together AI's category slot before someone else does?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time
github.com/rohansx/spnr
records.copy: Put Together AI in front of developers while their AI coding agent works — attested, agent-biddable terminal ad slots on spnr.

records.subject: Idea for Anthropic — ads in the AI-coding spinner
records.body:
Hi Jack,

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

The developers wiring up Anthropic's APIs basically live in that terminal — and on spnr, the agents acting for them can bid for your slot directly over x402.

We're lining up a handful of founding advertisers and Anthropic feels like a natural fit. It's the only *attested, anomaly-filtered* terminal ad slot — Ed25519-signed, hash-chained impressions — and the only one autonomous agents can bid on via x402. Blocks start at $1 per 1,000 impressions (5s each), open ascending auction, clicks at 50×. Fair warning: it's an early v0.1, so this is about reach + feedback, not real payouts yet.

Worth a quick 15 minutes to see if it's interesting for Anthropic?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time
github.com/rohansx/spnr

records.subject: Hugging Face × spnr — reach devs (and their agents) inside the terminal
records.body:
Hi Hugging Face team,

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

The developers wiring up Hugging Face's APIs basically live in that terminal — and on spnr, the agents acting for them can bid for your slot directly over x402.

It's the only *attested, anomaly-filtered* terminal ad slot: every impression is Ed25519-signed and BLAKE3 hash-chained, so you pay for verified developer attention, not self-reported counts. Pricing mirrors the category — 1 block = 1,000 impressions (5s each) from $1, open ascending auction, clicks billed at 50×. It's also the only slot autonomous agents can bid on directly via x402.

Straight up: spnr is an early v0.1 prototype and real-money settlement isn't wired up yet — so this is a founding-advertiser pilot, about reach and shaping the product, not a polished dashboard.

Would you be open to a 15-min call to claim Hugging Face's category slot before someone else does?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time
github.com/rohansx/spnr

records.subject: Idea for Qdrant — ads in the AI-coding spinner
records.body:
Hi Andre,

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

The developers wiring up Qdrant's APIs basically live in that terminal — and on spnr, the agents acting for them can bid for your slot directly over x402.

We're lining up a handful of founding advertisers and Qdrant feels like a natural fit. It's the only *attested, anomaly-filtered* terminal ad slot — Ed25519-signed, hash-chained impressions — and the only one autonomous agents can bid on via x402. Blocks start at $1 per 1,000 impressions (5s each), open ascending auction, clicks at 50×. Fair warning: it's an early v0.1, so this is about reach + feedback, not real payouts yet.

Worth a quick 15 minutes to see if it's interesting for Qdrant?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time
github.com/rohansx/spnr

The developers wiring up Upstash's APIs basically live in that terminal — and on spnr, the agents acting for them can bid for your slot directly over x402.

We're lining up a handful of founding advertisers and Upstash feels like a natural fit. It's the only *attested, anomaly-filtered* terminal ad slot — Ed25519-signed, hash-chained impressions — and the only one autonomous agents can bid on via x402. Blocks start at $1 per 1,000 impressions (5s each), open ascending auction, clicks at 50×. Fair warning: it's an early v0.1, so this is about reach + feedback, not real payouts yet.

Worth a quick 15 minutes to see if it's interesting for Upstash?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time
github.com/rohansx/spnr
records.copy: Put Upstash in front of developers while their AI coding agent works — attested, agent-biddable terminal ad slots on spnr.

Pricing is simple: 1 block = 1,000 impressions (5s each) from $1, clicks billed at 50×. Honest heads-up — spnr is an early v0.1 prototype, so this is a founding-partner pilot focused on reach and feedback.

Open to a quick 15-min call to get Qover's roles in front of developers where they actually spend their day?

records.subject: Put Domainsearcher in front of developers mid-build — spnr
records.body:
Hi Domainsearcher team,

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

spnr reaches an audience that's otherwise hard to buy: developers actively running AI coding tools, right inside the terminal.

It's the only *attested, anomaly-filtered* terminal ad slot: every impression is Ed25519-signed and BLAKE3 hash-chained, so you pay for verified developer attention, not self-reported counts. Pricing mirrors the category — 1 block = 1,000 impressions (5s each) from $1, open ascending auction, clicks billed at 50×. It's also the only slot autonomous agents can bid on directly via x402.

Straight up: spnr is an early v0.1 prototype and real-money settlement isn't wired up yet — so this is a founding-advertiser pilot, about reach and shaping the product, not a polished dashboard.

Would you be open to a 15-min call to claim Domainsearcher's category slot before someone else does?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time
github.com/rohansx/spnr

records.subject: Idea for Fabraix — ads in the AI-coding spinner
records.body:
Hi Zach,

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

spnr reaches an audience that's otherwise hard to buy: developers actively running AI coding tools, right inside the terminal.

We're lining up a handful of founding advertisers and Fabraix feels like a natural fit. It's the only *attested, anomaly-filtered* terminal ad slot — Ed25519-signed, hash-chained impressions — and the only one autonomous agents can bid on via x402. Blocks start at $1 per 1,000 impressions (5s each), open ascending auction, clicks at 50×. Fair warning: it's an early v0.1, so this is about reach + feedback, not real payouts yet.

Worth a quick 15 minutes to see if it's interesting for Fabraix?

records.subject: Hiring engineers? Reach them inside Claude Code — Pontoonglobal
records.body:
Hi Pontoonglobal team,

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

It's a way to put Pontoonglobal's open roles in front of engineers at the exact moment they're heads-down shipping code — active developers, not job-board browsers. Every impression is cryptographically attested, and the clickable status-line link can point straight at your careers page.

Pricing is simple: 1 block = 1,000 impressions (5s each) from $1, clicks billed at 50×. Honest heads-up — spnr is an early v0.1 prototype, so this is a founding-partner pilot focused on reach and feedback.

Open to a quick 15-min call to get Pontoonglobal's roles in front of developers where they actually spend their day?

— Rohan

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

spnr reaches an audience that's otherwise hard to buy: developers actively running AI coding tools, right inside the terminal.

It's the only *attested, anomaly-filtered* terminal ad slot: every impression is Ed25519-signed and BLAKE3 hash-chained, so you pay for verified developer attention, not self-reported counts. Pricing mirrors the category — 1 block = 1,000 impressions (5s each) from $1, open ascending auction, clicks billed at 50×. It's also the only slot autonomous agents can bid on directly via x402.

Straight up: spnr is an early v0.1 prototype and real-money settlement isn't wired up yet — so this is a founding-advertiser pilot, about reach and shaping the product, not a polished dashboard.

Would you be open to a 15-min call to claim Rustjobs's category slot before someone else does?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time
github.com/rohansx/spnr
records.copy: Put Rustjobs in front of developers while their AI coding agent works — attested, agent-biddable terminal ad slots on spnr.

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

spnr reaches an audience that's otherwise hard to buy: developers actively running AI coding tools, right inside the terminal.

It's the only *attested, anomaly-filtered* terminal ad slot: every impression is Ed25519-signed and BLAKE3 hash-chained, so you pay for verified developer attention, not self-reported counts. Pricing mirrors the category — 1 block = 1,000 impressions (5s each) from $1, open ascending auction, clicks billed at 50×. It's also the only slot autonomous agents can bid on directly via x402.

Straight up: spnr is an early v0.1 prototype and real-money settlement isn't wired up yet — so this is a founding-advertiser pilot, about reach and shaping the product, not a polished dashboard.

Would you be open to a 15-min call to claim Hyperspell's category slot before someone else does?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time
github.com/rohansx/spnr
records.copy: Put Hyperspell in front of developers while their AI coding agent works — attested, agent-biddable terminal ad slots on spnr.

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

spnr reaches an audience that's otherwise hard to buy: developers actively running AI coding tools, right inside the terminal.

We're lining up a handful of founding advertisers and Stealthmode feels like a natural fit. It's the only *attested, anomaly-filtered* terminal ad slot — Ed25519-signed, hash-chained impressions — and the only one autonomous agents can bid on via x402. Blocks start at $1 per 1,000 impressions (5s each), open ascending auction, clicks at 50×. Fair warning: it's an early v0.1, so this is about reach + feedback, not real payouts yet.

Worth a quick 15 minutes to see if it's interesting for Stealthmode?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time
github.com/rohansx/spnr
records.copy: Put Stealthmode in front of developers while their AI coding agent works — attested, agent-biddable terminal ad slots on spnr.

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

It's a way to put Dunzo's open roles in front of engineers at the exact moment they're heads-down shipping code — active developers, not job-board browsers. Every impression is cryptographically attested, and the clickable status-line link can point straight at your careers page.

Pricing is simple: 1 block = 1,000 impressions (5s each) from $1, clicks billed at 50×. Honest heads-up — spnr is an early v0.1 prototype, so this is a founding-partner pilot focused on reach and feedback.

Open to a quick 15-min call to get Dunzo's roles in front of developers where they actually spend their day?

— Rohan

records.subject: Idea for Stimuler — ads in the AI-coding spinner
records.body:
Hi Akshay,

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

spnr reaches an audience that's otherwise hard to buy: developers actively running AI coding tools, right inside the terminal.

We're lining up a handful of founding advertisers and Stimuler feels like a natural fit. It's the only *attested, anomaly-filtered* terminal ad slot — Ed25519-signed, hash-chained impressions — and the only one autonomous agents can bid on via x402. Blocks start at $1 per 1,000 impressions (5s each), open ascending auction, clicks at 50×. Fair warning: it's an early v0.1, so this is about reach + feedback, not real payouts yet.

Worth a quick 15 minutes to see if it's interesting for Stimuler?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time
github.com/rohansx/spnr
records.copy: Put Stimuler in front of developers while their AI coding agent works — attested, agent-biddable terminal ad slots on spnr.

records.subject: Atlan × spnr — reach devs (and their agents) inside the terminal
records.body:
Hi Atlan team,

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

The developers wiring up Atlan's APIs basically live in that terminal — and on spnr, the agents acting for them can bid for your slot directly over x402.

It's the only *attested, anomaly-filtered* terminal ad slot: every impression is Ed25519-signed and BLAKE3 hash-chained, so you pay for verified developer attention, not self-reported counts. Pricing mirrors the category — 1 block = 1,000 impressions (5s each) from $1, open ascending auction, clicks billed at 50×. It's also the only slot autonomous agents can bid on directly via x402.

Straight up: spnr is an early v0.1 prototype and real-money settlement isn't wired up yet — so this is a founding-advertiser pilot, about reach and shaping the product, not a polished dashboard.

Would you be open to a 15-min call to claim Atlan's category slot before someone else does?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time
github.com/rohansx/spnr
records.copy: Put Atlan in front of developers while their AI coding agent works — attested, agent-biddable terminal ad slots on spnr.

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

spnr reaches an audience that's otherwise hard to buy: developers actively running AI coding tools, right inside the terminal.

We're lining up a handful of founding advertisers and Ananet feels like a natural fit. It's the only *attested, anomaly-filtered* terminal ad slot — Ed25519-signed, hash-chained impressions — and the only one autonomous agents can bid on via x402. Blocks start at $1 per 1,000 impressions (5s each), open ascending auction, clicks at 50×. Fair warning: it's an early v0.1, so this is about reach + feedback, not real payouts yet.

Worth a quick 15 minutes to see if it's interesting for Ananet?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time
github.com/rohansx/spnr

records.subject: Put Sideshift in front of developers mid-build — spnr
records.body:
Hi Sideshift team,

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

records.subject: Hiring engineers? Reach them inside Claude Code — Tamas
records.body:
Hi Tamas team,

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

It's a way to put Tamas's open roles in front of engineers at the exact moment they're heads-down shipping code — active developers, not job-board browsers. Every impression is cryptographically attested, and the clickable status-line link can point straight at your careers page.

Pricing is simple: 1 block = 1,000 impressions (5s each) from $1, clicks billed at 50×. Honest heads-up — spnr is an early v0.1 prototype, so this is a founding-partner pilot focused on reach and feedback.

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

Your earliest adopters are builders running AI coding agents all day — which is exactly who spnr puts Midjourney in front of.

It's the only *attested, anomaly-filtered* terminal ad slot: every impression is Ed25519-signed and BLAKE3 hash-chained, so you pay for verified developer attention, not self-reported counts. Pricing mirrors the category — 1 block = 1,000 impressions (5s each) from $1, open ascending auction, clicks billed at 50×. It's also the only slot autonomous agents can bid on directly via x402.

Straight up: spnr is an early v0.1 prototype and real-money settlement isn't wired up yet — so this is a founding-advertiser pilot, about reach and shaping the product, not a polished dashboard.

Would you be open to a 15-min call to claim Midjourney's category slot before someone else does?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time
github.com/rohansx/spnr
records.copy: Put Midjourney in front of developers while their AI coding agent works — attested, agent-biddable terminal ad slots on spnr.

records.body:
Hi Tiger Analytics team,

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

It's a way to put Tiger Analytics's open roles in front of engineers at the exact moment they're heads-down shipping code — active developers, not job-board browsers. Every impression is cryptographically attested, and the clickable status-line link can point straight at your careers page.

records.subject: Put EPAM in front of developers mid-build — spnr
records.body:
Hi EPAM team,

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

spnr reaches an audience that's otherwise hard to buy: developers actively running AI coding tools, right inside the terminal.

It's the only *attested, anomaly-filtered* terminal ad slot: every impression is Ed25519-signed and BLAKE3 hash-chained, so you pay for verified developer attention, not self-reported counts. Pricing mirrors the category — 1 block = 1,000 impressions (5s each) from $1, open ascending auction, clicks billed at 50×. It's also the only slot autonomous agents can bid on directly via x402.

Straight up: spnr is an early v0.1 prototype and real-money settlement isn't wired up yet — so this is a founding-advertiser pilot, about reach and shaping the product, not a polished dashboard.

Would you be open to a 15-min call to claim EPAM's category slot before someone else does?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time
github.com/rohansx/spnr

records.subject: Idea for Doanything — ads in the AI-coding spinner
records.body:
Hi Garrett,

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

spnr reaches an audience that's otherwise hard to buy: developers actively running AI coding tools, right inside the terminal.

We're lining up a handful of founding advertisers and Doanything feels like a natural fit. It's the only *attested, anomaly-filtered* terminal ad slot — Ed25519-signed, hash-chained impressions — and the only one autonomous agents can bid on via x402. Blocks start at $1 per 1,000 impressions (5s each), open ascending auction, clicks at 50×. Fair warning: it's an early v0.1, so this is about reach + feedback, not real payouts yet.

Worth a quick 15 minutes to see if it's interesting for Doanything?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time
github.com/rohansx/spnr

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

spnr reaches an audience that's otherwise hard to buy: developers actively running AI coding tools, right inside the terminal.

We're lining up a handful of founding advertisers and Freedomrobotics feels like a natural fit. It's the only *attested, anomaly-filtered* terminal ad slot — Ed25519-signed, hash-chained impressions — and the only one autonomous agents can bid on via x402. Blocks start at $1 per 1,000 impressions (5s each), open ascending auction, clicks at 50×. Fair warning: it's an early v0.1, so this is about reach + feedback, not real payouts yet.

Worth a quick 15 minutes to see if it's interesting for Freedomrobotics?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time
github.com/rohansx/spnr

records.subject: Idea for Eager — ads in the AI-coding spinner
records.body:
Hi Zack,

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

spnr reaches an audience that's otherwise hard to buy: developers actively running AI coding tools, right inside the terminal.

We're lining up a handful of founding advertisers and Eager feels like a natural fit. It's the only *attested, anomaly-filtered* terminal ad slot — Ed25519-signed, hash-chained impressions — and the only one autonomous agents can bid on via x402. Blocks start at $1 per 1,000 impressions (5s each), open ascending auction, clicks at 50×. Fair warning: it's an early v0.1, so this is about reach + feedback, not real payouts yet.

Worth a quick 15 minutes to see if it's interesting for Eager?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time
github.com/rohansx/spnr
records.copy: Put Eager in front of developers while their AI coding agent works — attested, agent-biddable terminal ad slots on spnr.

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

spnr reaches an audience that's otherwise hard to buy: developers actively running AI coding tools, right inside the terminal.

We're lining up a handful of founding advertisers and Kubermatic feels like a natural fit. It's the only *attested, anomaly-filtered* terminal ad slot — Ed25519-signed, hash-chained impressions — and the only one autonomous agents can bid on via x402. Blocks start at $1 per 1,000 impressions (5s each), open ascending auction, clicks at 50×. Fair warning: it's an early v0.1, so this is about reach + feedback, not real payouts yet.

Worth a quick 15 minutes to see if it's interesting for Kubermatic?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time
github.com/rohansx/spnr
records.copy: Put Kubermatic in front of developers while their AI coding agent works — attested, agent-biddable terminal ad slots on spnr.

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

spnr reaches an audience that's otherwise hard to buy: developers actively running AI coding tools, right inside the terminal.

We're lining up a handful of founding advertisers and Onepanta feels like a natural fit. It's the only *attested, anomaly-filtered* terminal ad slot — Ed25519-signed, hash-chained impressions — and the only one autonomous agents can bid on via x402. Blocks start at $1 per 1,000 impressions (5s each), open ascending auction, clicks at 50×. Fair warning: it's an early v0.1, so this is about reach + feedback, not real payouts yet.

Worth a quick 15 minutes to see if it's interesting for Onepanta?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time
github.com/rohansx/spnr
records.copy: Put Onepanta in front of developers while their AI coding agent works — attested, agent-biddable terminal ad slots on spnr.

records.subject: Put Sequentech in front of developers mid-build — spnr
records.body:
Hi Sequentech team,

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

spnr reaches an audience that's otherwise hard to buy: developers actively running AI coding tools, right inside the terminal.

It's the only *attested, anomaly-filtered* terminal ad slot: every impression is Ed25519-signed and BLAKE3 hash-chained, so you pay for verified developer attention, not self-reported counts. Pricing mirrors the category — 1 block = 1,000 impressions (5s each) from $1, open ascending auction, clicks billed at 50×. It's also the only slot autonomous agents can bid on directly via x402.

Straight up: spnr is an early v0.1 prototype and real-money settlement isn't wired up yet — so this is a founding-advertiser pilot, about reach and shaping the product, not a polished dashboard.

Would you be open to a 15-min call to claim Sequentech's category slot before someone else does?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time
github.com/rohansx/spnr
records.copy: Put Sequentech in front of developers while their AI coding agent works — attested, agent-biddable terminal ad slots on spnr.

records.subject: Put Realroots in front of developers mid-build — spnr
records.body:
Hi Realroots team,

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

Your earliest adopters are builders running AI coding agents all day — which is exactly who spnr puts Realroots in front of.

It's the only *attested, anomaly-filtered* terminal ad slot: every impression is Ed25519-signed and BLAKE3 hash-chained, so you pay for verified developer attention, not self-reported counts. Pricing mirrors the category — 1 block = 1,000 impressions (5s each) from $1, open ascending auction, clicks billed at 50×. It's also the only slot autonomous agents can bid on directly via x402.

Straight up: spnr is an early v0.1 prototype and real-money settlement isn't wired up yet — so this is a founding-advertiser pilot, about reach and shaping the product, not a polished dashboard.

records.subject: Hiring engineers? Reach them inside Claude Code — Zest
records.body:
Hi Zest team,

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

It's a way to put Zest's open roles in front of engineers at the exact moment they're heads-down shipping code — active developers, not job-board browsers. Every impression is cryptographically attested, and the clickable status-line link can point straight at your careers page.

Pricing is simple: 1 block = 1,000 impressions (5s each) from $1, clicks billed at 50×. Honest heads-up — spnr is an early v0.1 prototype, so this is a founding-partner pilot focused on reach and feedback.

Open to a quick 15-min call to get Zest's roles in front of developers where they actually spend their day?

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

It's a way to put Space's open roles in front of engineers at the exact moment they're heads-down shipping code — active developers, not job-board browsers. Every impression is cryptographically attested, and the clickable status-line link can point straight at your careers page.

Pricing is simple: 1 block = 1,000 impressions (5s each) from $1, clicks billed at 50×. Honest heads-up — spnr is an early v0.1 prototype, so this is a founding-partner pilot focused on reach and feedback.

Open to a quick 15-min call to get Space's roles in front of developers where they actually spend their day?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time
github.com/rohansx/spnr

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

spnr reaches an audience that's otherwise hard to buy: developers actively running AI coding tools, right inside the terminal.

It's the only *attested, anomaly-filtered* terminal ad slot: every impression is Ed25519-signed and BLAKE3 hash-chained, so you pay for verified developer attention, not self-reported counts. Pricing mirrors the category — 1 block = 1,000 impressions (5s each) from $1, open ascending auction, clicks billed at 50×. It's also the only slot autonomous agents can bid on directly via x402.

Straight up: spnr is an early v0.1 prototype and real-money settlement isn't wired up yet — so this is a founding-advertiser pilot, about reach and shaping the product, not a polished dashboard.

Would you be open to a 15-min call to claim Aonehire's category slot before someone else does?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time
github.com/rohansx/spnr
records.copy: Put Aonehire in front of developers while their AI coding agent works — attested, agent-biddable terminal ad slots on spnr.

records.subject: Idea for Doclabs — ads in the AI-coding spinner
records.body:
Hi Srini,

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

Your earliest adopters are builders running AI coding agents all day — which is exactly who spnr puts Doclabs in front of.

We're lining up a handful of founding advertisers and Doclabs feels like a natural fit. It's the only *attested, anomaly-filtered* terminal ad slot — Ed25519-signed, hash-chained impressions — and the only one autonomous agents can bid on via x402. Blocks start at $1 per 1,000 impressions (5s each), open ascending auction, clicks at 50×. Fair warning: it's an early v0.1, so this is about reach + feedback, not real payouts yet.

Worth a quick 15 minutes to see if it's interesting for Doclabs?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time

records.subject: Put Kpmg in front of developers mid-build — spnr
records.body:
Hi Kpmg team,

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

spnr reaches an audience that's otherwise hard to buy: developers actively running AI coding tools, right inside the terminal.

It's the only *attested, anomaly-filtered* terminal ad slot: every impression is Ed25519-signed and BLAKE3 hash-chained, so you pay for verified developer attention, not self-reported counts. Pricing mirrors the category — 1 block = 1,000 impressions (5s each) from $1, open ascending auction, clicks billed at 50×. It's also the only slot autonomous agents can bid on directly via x402.

Straight up: spnr is an early v0.1 prototype and real-money settlement isn't wired up yet — so this is a founding-advertiser pilot, about reach and shaping the product, not a polished dashboard.

Would you be open to a 15-min call to claim Kpmg's category slot before someone else does?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time
github.com/rohansx/spnr

records.subject: Idea for Adobe — ads in the AI-coding spinner
records.body:
Hi Skatkar,

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

spnr reaches an audience that's otherwise hard to buy: developers actively running AI coding tools, right inside the terminal.

We're lining up a handful of founding advertisers and Adobe feels like a natural fit. It's the only *attested, anomaly-filtered* terminal ad slot — Ed25519-signed, hash-chained impressions — and the only one autonomous agents can bid on via x402. Blocks start at $1 per 1,000 impressions (5s each), open ascending auction, clicks at 50×. Fair warning: it's an early v0.1, so this is about reach + feedback, not real payouts yet.

Worth a quick 15 minutes to see if it's interesting for Adobe?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time
github.com/rohansx/spnr

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

spnr reaches an audience that's otherwise hard to buy: developers actively running AI coding tools, right inside the terminal.

We're lining up a handful of founding advertisers and Watttime feels like a natural fit. It's the only *attested, anomaly-filtered* terminal ad slot — Ed25519-signed, hash-chained impressions — and the only one autonomous agents can bid on via x402. Blocks start at $1 per 1,000 impressions (5s each), open ascending auction, clicks at 50×. Fair warning: it's an early v0.1, so this is about reach + feedback, not real payouts yet.

Worth a quick 15 minutes to see if it's interesting for Watttime?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time
github.com/rohansx/spnr
records.copy: Put Watttime in front of developers while their AI coding agent works — attested, agent-biddable terminal ad slots on spnr.

records.subject: Put Gy in front of developers mid-build — spnr
records.body:
Hi Gy team,

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

spnr reaches an audience that's otherwise hard to buy: developers actively running AI coding tools, right inside the terminal.

It's the only *attested, anomaly-filtered* terminal ad slot: every impression is Ed25519-signed and BLAKE3 hash-chained, so you pay for verified developer attention, not self-reported counts. Pricing mirrors the category — 1 block = 1,000 impressions (5s each) from $1, open ascending auction, clicks billed at 50×. It's also the only slot autonomous agents can bid on directly via x402.

Straight up: spnr is an early v0.1 prototype and real-money settlement isn't wired up yet — so this is a founding-advertiser pilot, about reach and shaping the product, not a polished dashboard.

Would you be open to a 15-min call to claim Gy's category slot before someone else does?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time
github.com/rohansx/spnr
records.copy: Put Gy in front of developers while their AI coding agent works — attested, agent-biddable terminal ad slots on spnr.

records.subject: Idea for Landeed — ads in the AI-coding spinner
records.body:
Hi Sanjay,

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

spnr reaches an audience that's otherwise hard to buy: developers actively running AI coding tools, right inside the terminal.

We're lining up a handful of founding advertisers and Landeed feels like a natural fit. It's the only *attested, anomaly-filtered* terminal ad slot — Ed25519-signed, hash-chained impressions — and the only one autonomous agents can bid on via x402. Blocks start at $1 per 1,000 impressions (5s each), open ascending auction, clicks at 50×. Fair warning: it's an early v0.1, so this is about reach + feedback, not real payouts yet.

Worth a quick 15 minutes to see if it's interesting for Landeed?

— Rohan

records.subject: Put Valuecoders in front of developers mid-build — spnr
records.body:
Hi Valuecoders team,

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

spnr reaches an audience that's otherwise hard to buy: developers actively running AI coding tools, right inside the terminal.

It's the only *attested, anomaly-filtered* terminal ad slot: every impression is Ed25519-signed and BLAKE3 hash-chained, so you pay for verified developer attention, not self-reported counts. Pricing mirrors the category — 1 block = 1,000 impressions (5s each) from $1, open ascending auction, clicks billed at 50×. It's also the only slot autonomous agents can bid on directly via x402.

Straight up: spnr is an early v0.1 prototype and real-money settlement isn't wired up yet — so this is a founding-advertiser pilot, about reach and shaping the product, not a polished dashboard.

Would you be open to a 15-min call to claim Valuecoders's category slot before someone else does?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time
github.com/rohansx/spnr
records.copy: Put Valuecoders in front of developers while their AI coding agent works — attested, agent-biddable terminal ad slots on spnr.

It's the only *attested, anomaly-filtered* terminal ad slot: every impression is Ed25519-signed and BLAKE3 hash-chained, so you pay for verified developer attention, not self-reported counts. Pricing mirrors the category — 1 block = 1,000 impressions (5s each) from $1, open ascending auction, clicks billed at 50×. It's also the only slot autonomous agents can bid on directly via x402.

Straight up: spnr is an early v0.1 prototype and real-money settlement isn't wired up yet — so this is a founding-advertiser pilot, about reach and shaping the product, not a polished dashboard.

Would you be open to a 15-min call to claim Opptra's category slot before someone else does?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time
github.com/rohansx/spnr
records.copy: Put Opptra in front of developers while their AI coding agent works — attested, agent-biddable terminal ad slots on spnr.

records.subject: Idea for Moneyview — ads in the AI-coding spinner
records.body:
Hi Roopna,

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

spnr reaches an audience that's otherwise hard to buy: developers actively running AI coding tools, right inside the terminal.

We're lining up a handful of founding advertisers and Moneyview feels like a natural fit. It's the only *attested, anomaly-filtered* terminal ad slot — Ed25519-signed, hash-chained impressions — and the only one autonomous agents can bid on via x402. Blocks start at $1 per 1,000 impressions (5s each), open ascending auction, clicks at 50×. Fair warning: it's an early v0.1, so this is about reach + feedback, not real payouts yet.

Worth a quick 15 minutes to see if it's interesting for Moneyview?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time

records.subject: Hiring engineers? Reach them inside Claude Code — Blueai
records.body:
Hi Blueai team,

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

It's a way to put Blueai's open roles in front of engineers at the exact moment they're heads-down shipping code — active developers, not job-board browsers. Every impression is cryptographically attested, and the clickable status-line link can point straight at your careers page.

Pricing is simple: 1 block = 1,000 impressions (5s each) from $1, clicks billed at 50×. Honest heads-up — spnr is an early v0.1 prototype, so this is a founding-partner pilot focused on reach and feedback.

Open to a quick 15-min call to get Blueai's roles in front of developers where they actually spend their day?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time
github.com/rohansx/spnr
records.copy: Get Blueai's open roles in front of engineers mid-build — attested developer attention on spnr's terminal ad network.

records.subject: Hiring engineers? Reach them inside Claude Code — Saia
records.body:
Hi Saia team,

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

It's a way to put Saia's open roles in front of engineers at the exact moment they're heads-down shipping code — active developers, not job-board browsers. Every impression is cryptographically attested, and the clickable status-line link can point straight at your careers page.

Pricing is simple: 1 block = 1,000 impressions (5s each) from $1, clicks billed at 50×. Honest heads-up — spnr is an early v0.1 prototype, so this is a founding-partner pilot focused on reach and feedback.

Open to a quick 15-min call to get Saia's roles in front of developers where they actually spend their day?

records.subject: Idea for CloudSEK — ads in the AI-coding spinner
records.body:
Hi Rahul,

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

CloudSEK already lives where developers work; spnr extends that reach into their agent's idle wait-time, where nothing else is competing for attention.

We're lining up a handful of founding advertisers and CloudSEK feels like a natural fit. It's the only *attested, anomaly-filtered* terminal ad slot — Ed25519-signed, hash-chained impressions — and the only one autonomous agents can bid on via x402. Blocks start at $1 per 1,000 impressions (5s each), open ascending auction, clicks at 50×. Fair warning: it's an early v0.1, so this is about reach + feedback, not real payouts yet.

Worth a quick 15 minutes to see if it's interesting for CloudSEK?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time
github.com/rohansx/spnr
records.copy: Put CloudSEK in front of developers while their AI coding agent works — attested, agent-biddable terminal ad slots on spnr.

records.subject: Cloud × spnr — reach devs (and their agents) inside the terminal
records.body:
Hi Cloud team,

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

The developers wiring up Cloud's APIs basically live in that terminal — and on spnr, the agents acting for them can bid for your slot directly over x402.

It's the only *attested, anomaly-filtered* terminal ad slot: every impression is Ed25519-signed and BLAKE3 hash-chained, so you pay for verified developer attention, not self-reported counts. Pricing mirrors the category — 1 block = 1,000 impressions (5s each) from $1, open ascending auction, clicks billed at 50×. It's also the only slot autonomous agents can bid on directly via x402.

Straight up: spnr is an early v0.1 prototype and real-money settlement isn't wired up yet — so this is a founding-advertiser pilot, about reach and shaping the product, not a polished dashboard.

Would you be open to a 15-min call to claim Cloud's category slot before someone else does?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time
github.com/rohansx/spnr
records.copy: Put Cloud in front of developers while their AI coding agent works — attested, agent-biddable terminal ad slots on spnr.

Pricing mirrors the category — 1 block = 1,000 impressions (5s each) from $1, open ascending auction, clicks billed at 50×. It's also the only slot autonomous agents can bid on directly via x402.

Straight up: spnr is an early v0.1 prototype and real-money settlement isn't wired up yet — so this is a founding-advertiser pilot, about reach and shaping the product, not a polished dashboard.

Would you be open to a 15-min call to claim Vivatechnology's category slot before someone else does?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time

records.subject: Put Resellerclub in front of developers mid-build — spnr
records.body:
Hi Resellerclub team,

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

spnr reaches an audience that's otherwise hard to buy: developers actively running AI coding tools, right inside the terminal.

It's the only *attested, anomaly-filtered* terminal ad slot: every impression is Ed25519-signed and BLAKE3 hash-chained, so you pay for verified developer attention, not self-reported counts. Pricing mirrors the category — 1 block = 1,000 impressions (5s each) from $1, open ascending auction, clicks billed at 50×. It's also the only slot autonomous agents can bid on directly via x402.

Straight up: spnr is an early v0.1 prototype and real-money settlement isn't wired up yet — so this is a founding-advertiser pilot, about reach and shaping the product, not a polished dashboard.

Would you be open to a 15-min call to claim Resellerclub's category slot before someone else does?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time
github.com/rohansx/spnr

Blocks start at $1 per 1,000 impressions (5s each), open ascending auction, clicks at 50×. Fair warning: it's an early v0.1, so this is about reach + feedback, not real payouts yet.

Worth a quick 15 minutes to see if it's interesting for Cloudx?

— Rohan

records.subject: Hiring engineers? Reach them inside Claude Code — Squadstack
records.body:
Hi Squadstack team,

I'm Rohan, building spnr — an open, terminal-native ad network that monetizes the "spinner" wait-time in AI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex. While a developer's agent is working, the spinner shows a sponsored line plus a clickable earnings status-line.

It's a way to put Squadstack's open roles in front of engineers at the exact moment they're heads-down shipping code — active developers, not job-board browsers. Every impression is cryptographically attested, and the clickable status-line link can point straight at your careers page.

Pricing is simple: 1 block = 1,000 impressions (5s each) from $1, clicks billed at 50×. Honest heads-up — spnr is an early v0.1 prototype, so this is a founding-partner pilot focused on reach and feedback.

Open to a quick 15-min call to get Squadstack's roles in front of developers where they actually spend their day?

— Rohan
spnr · the open ad network for AI-coding-agent wait-time
